description: Use Logto (self-hosted or cloud) as the auth provider for Convex React apps: an ID-token OIDC bridge plus a signed webhook user-sync.
rules: convex-logto bridges Logto's OIDC ID token into Convex auth. It deliberately uses the ID token, not Logto access tokens (which are typed `at+jwt` and rejected by Convex).

Import server helpers (logtoAuthConfig, logtoConfigQuery, logtoSync, registerLogtoWebhook, verifyLogtoSignature) from `convex-logto`. Import React helpers (ConvexLogtoProvider, useLogtoAuth) from `convex-logto/react`, which is ESM-only.

Configure Logto via Convex deployment env vars: LOGTO_ENDPOINT, LOGTO_APP_ID (and LOGTO_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_KEY for webhook sync). The frontend needs no Logto config of its own when it pulls config from the backend via logtoConfigQuery + the ConvexLogtoProvider `configQuery` prop.
There is no JWT template, algorithm, or JWKS URL to configure — Convex auto-discovers them over OIDC (Logto signs ID tokens with ES384).
Peer dependencies: convex (required), plus react and @logto/react on the frontend. Install with `npm i convex-logto @logto/react`.
